Period:
Regulatory Transformation

Regulatory Transformation: navigating emerging risks, driving AI adoption and reducing regulatory burden

Regulators are facing a shifting operating environment as technology, markets, environmental pressures and societal expectations evolve faster than traditional regulatory frameworks. Fragmented data and limited intelligence continue to make risk prioritisation difficult.

There is a growing opportunity to use advanced analytics to better target regulatory effort. Many regulators are still working to integrate data across systems, uplift analytical capability and embed insights into everyday decision-making.

AI adoption is also becoming a core regulatory transformation issue. Regulators are seeking to balance opportunities for automation and improved risk identification with the need to manage governance, transparency, bias, legal risk, privacy and public confidence.

At the same time, governments are under pressure to reduce red tape, make faster decisions and manage fiscal constraints. This is driving interest in regulatory consolidation, shared-service models and more consistent approaches to regulatory delivery, while maintaining fairness and strong outcomes.

Regulation Hub Intelligence Brief

AI Brief 151 articles across 8 categories

Three regulator-investment themes dominate the fortnight: AUSTRAC has escalated the financial crime agenda with refreshed money laundering and proliferation financing snapshots plus a formal AML/CTF investigation into Tabcorp; ASIC and APRA are simultaneously signalling AI governance uplift, sustainability-reporting readiness ahead of 30 June 2026, and a 'regulatory clutter' overhaul (REP 830); and the Commonwealth has frozen new overseas student CRICOS registrations for 12 months while ATEC stands up under its first Letter of Expectations. Named clients in the news span Tabcorp (AUSTRAC enforcement), the former Xinja chair (APRA disqualification), RACP and president-elect Dr Sharmila Chandran (ACNC suspension), Transgrid (AER System Security Roadmap CPA), the Victorian Lottery Corporation (VAGO licence review), and ANU (adverse-findings probe) — each a defensible governance, probity or remediation pursuit. Concurrently NDIS reform (tightened access tests, NT fraud crackdown, 2026-27 Budget measures) and SIRA's two-year NSW injury-provider reshape are creating provider-integrity and operating-model demand, while iTnews confirms new federal billions sustaining core gov tech programs. Partners should mobilise the Forensic and Risk Consulting bench around Tabcorp-adjacent gaming/wagering AML remediation and Tranche 2 VASP readiness this week, and stand up a combined APRA-FAR plus ASIC sustainability-assurance readiness offer for ADIs and RSE licensees before 30 June. In parallel, position the Public Sector probity and AI-assurance benches against the VAGO Lottery review, the CRICOS pause and the ATEC stand-up, and package a governance-uplift play for medical colleges and health peak bodies off the RACP precedent. BD effort this fortnight should concentrate on AML/CTF remediation, sustainability-assurance readiness, and public-sector probity reviews — not generic regulatory monitoring.

Cross-category themes

Across all categories, regulators are simultaneously simplifying frameworks while sharpening enforcement — with converging scrutiny on AI/algorithmic accountability, governance and director accountability, AML/financial crime, and supply chain/critical infrastructure resilience driving material compliance uplift across ASIC, APRA, AUSTRAC, TEQSA, ACNC, and sector regulators. This dual pressure of streamlined-but-stricter regulation, combined with major budget-driven reforms (NDIS, sustainability reporting, foreign investment, payments modernisation) and intensified personal liability exposure, is creating concentrated advisory demand for governance reviews, regulator engagement strategy, and integrated risk uplift programs spanning AI, ESG, financial crime, and critical infrastructure obligations.

Public Sector

AI Brief15 articles

ASIC and APRA are simultaneously signalling regulator-side investment in AI governance and a 'regulatory clutter' digital/guidance overhaul, while OAIC consults on automated decision-making transparency and WGEA publishes its own AI transparency statement — a clear pattern of public sector bodies needing AI assurance uplift and policy redesign support. Enforcement and review pull-through is concrete: the Victorian Auditor-General is opening the Lottery Corporation 40-year licence extension, Consumer Affairs Victoria has landed a $1m+ rental breach penalty, and the Commonwealth has frozen new overseas student provider registrations for 12 months — each creating defensible probity, value-for-money and program-redesign mandates. Victoria's new NDA restrictions in workplace sexual harassment matters and the QLD housing infrastructure deal round out a fortnight where regulator capability, AI risk frameworks and program assurance are all in play. Position the Public Sector AI assurance and probity-review benches now.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Public Sector AI lead and VAGO relationship partner this fortnight to pre-position for the Lottery Corporation probity review and ASIC/APRA/OAIC AI governance work; package a combined 'regulator AI transparency + automated decision-making assurance' offering ahead of the OAIC consultation close.

Energy, Mining & Property

AI Brief19 articles

WA's renewable energy regulatory overhaul (Ashurst) and the AER's Transgrid System Security Roadmap CPA signal material regulator investment in grid security and project approvals capability, creating immediate pursuit leads for transmission proponents and developers navigating new WA approvals pathways. The MCA's Critical Minerals Inquiry testimony and Geoscience Australia's new critical minerals facilities point to a sustained Commonwealth capability build that PEPI can monetise through policy advisory and supply-chain assurance. Concurrently, NSW EPA's $1.2M illegal dumping enforcement fund, VBA's incoming Home Warranty insurance scheme, and HIA's Budget response on housing supply/taxation open risk, assurance and operating-model advisory angles across property and waste. Advisory hook: position the energy regulatory team for WA renewables transition support and stand up a critical minerals supply-chain assurance proposition before Senate Estimates.

Implications for KPMGBrief the Energy & Natural Resources partner this fortnight to scope a WA renewables approvals advisory offer ahead of the new regime, and align the Mining lead with Geoscience Australia and MCA contacts to position KPMG for critical minerals supply-chain and assurance work tied to the APH inquiry. In parallel, prep the Property partner on VBA's Home Warranty insurance launch — there's a near-term operating model and assurance pursuit with the VBA and builder clients.

Health, Ageing & Human Services

AI Brief21 articles

ACNC's suspension of RACP president-elect Dr Sharmila Chandran signals an escalation in charities-regulator scrutiny of medical college governance and overseas conflicts of interest — a clear pull-through for KPMG governance, probity and board-effectiveness reviews across health peak bodies. The NDIS reform package (tightened access tests, fraud crackdown in NT, and Budget 2026-27 disability/aged-care measures) is creating concurrent demand for provider integrity assurance, eligibility-decision assurance frameworks, and workforce capability uplift as 'exhaust all appropriate treatments' rules reshape provider operating models. TGA's Pregnancy database refresh and Indonesia halal certification changes open a narrow but high-margin lane for life-sciences regulatory operating-model advice for export-oriented sponsors.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: Brief the Health & Human Services partner this fortnight to mobilise an NDIS provider-integrity and eligibility-assurance proposition ahead of the Senate passage, and pre-position the governance bench with RACP and adjacent medical colleges on ACNC-aligned conflicts-of-interest reviews. Pair with a Life Sciences regulatory note to TGA-exposed clients on the Indonesia halal pathway.

Financial Services

AI Brief26 articles

APRA's six-year disqualification of the former Xinja chair and ASIC's escalating enforcement pipeline (UGC adviser ban extension, Arandez 5-year custodial sentence, MIS audit surveillance) signal a sustained capability uplift in supervisory and forensic functions — partners should read this as both a regulator tech/data-investment tell and a private-side governance remediation pull. ASIC REP 830 (regulatory simplification) and the sustainability reporting early observations ahead of 30 June 2026 open a clear assurance and reporting-readiness lane, while Treasury's payments modernisation, the YFYS performance test review, and ABA/CDR-ATO data expansion create concurrent transformation mandates across banks, super trustees and payments incumbents. The FSC's foreign investment streamlining position and the discretionary trust minimum tax proposal add a tax/structuring overlay that PEPI can package with deals advisory. Concrete angle: a combined APRA-FAR + ASIC sustainability-assurance readiness offer for ADIs and RSE licensees, anchored on board-accountability remediation.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the FS Assurance and Risk & Regulatory partners this fortnight to package a board-level 'AFSL/ADI accountability + sustainability reporting dry-run' offer ahead of 30 June 2026, and have the Payments lead pre-position with Treasury-exposed clients on ISO 20022 and CDR-ATO scope expansion before the next Banking sector roundtable.

National Security & Justice

AI Brief21 articles

AUSTRAC has triple-stacked the financial crime agenda this fortnight with refreshed money laundering and proliferation financing risk snapshots plus a formal AML/CTF investigation into Tabcorp, while eSafety is escalating enforcement against 'nudify' services and broadening its remit into online harm in sport. Federal investment signals are concrete: iTnews confirms new billions to sustain core gov tech programs, a new federal counter-terror coordination role has been stood up, and ABF's Operation Pristis 2026 plus AFP-Fiji Pacific policing summits point to capability uplift across border and transnational crime. ASIC's finfluencer crackdown and The Australian's illicit tobacco prosecution data round out a clear pursuit window for AML/CTF remediation, Tranche 2 readiness for VASPs, and Trust & Safety advisory. Position the Forensic and Risk Consulting benches now — Tabcorp-adjacent gaming/wagering clients will be re-papering AML programs within weeks.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: Brief the Forensic and Financial Crime partners before next fortnight to mobilise a Tabcorp-style AML/CTF health-check offer across wagering, VASP and Tranche 2 clients, and align the Gov Tech partner to the new Commonwealth funding envelope flagged by iTnews and the counter-terror coordinator role.

Education

AI Brief27 articles

Regulator investment is concentrating on integrity, capability and standards enforcement: DEWR has paused new CRICOS course registrations at private VET/ELICOS providers for 12 months, TEQSA has accepted a voluntary undertaking and released refreshed academic-integrity and contract-cheating investigator guides, and the newly-legislated Australian Tertiary Education Commission has issued its first Letter of Expectations setting the agenda for sector reform. The ANU adverse-findings probe and the AFR critique of TEQSA's intervention create immediate governance-uplift pursuit leads, while the 2026–27 DEWR Portfolio Budget Statements signal funding flowing into ATEC stand-up, integrity tech and provider oversight. Concrete advisory angle: a combined governance review + academic-integrity assurance offering for at-risk providers facing TEQSA undertakings or CRICOS pause exposure.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: Higher Ed partner should brief ANU and Group of Eight CFOs this fortnight on a governance + integrity assurance package responding to the TEQSA voluntary undertaking precedent, and engage ATEC and DEWR contacts on operating-model and oversight design before the Letter of Expectations work program firms up.

Transport

AI Brief19 articles

Regulator tech and capability investment is the dominant signal this fortnight: ETCS rollout is reshaping national rail signalling, CASA is standing up AusSORA BVLOS approvals plus PEG-based examiner uplift, and TAC is scaling camera enforcement tech for National Road Safety Week. Concurrently, AMSA's Marine Orders 12/32 amendments and the Liberian-flagged bulker port ban open assurance and crew-welfare compliance pursuits, while NTC's Bulk Tanker Day 2026 and CASA's legacy airworthiness directive review signal productivity and regulatory-burden engagements. The ATSB aerial firefighting safety analysis and ACT fuel supply disruption bill round out a clear advisory agenda around safety assurance, resilience and digital regulator uplift.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Transport & Infrastructure partner ahead of the next ARTC/state rail client touchpoint on ETCS program assurance and digital signalling delivery risk, and position the maritime assurance bench against AMSA's Marine Orders 12/32 and the crew-exploitation enforcement posture. Line up CASA-facing drone/BVLOS and examiner-uplift advisory before AusSORA pathway formalises.

Defence

AI Brief3 articles

WorkSec is positioning itself as the compliance backbone for Defence industry personnel security, with the first charges laid under the SAMS Act and a new AIDN partnership signalling regulator appetite for enforcement and SME uplift across the Defence supply chain. In parallel, the independent review of the SOCI Act has landed with regulated entities now needing to reassess critical infrastructure obligations, risk management programs and incident response postures. The combined signal is a clear pull-through for personnel security governance reviews, SAMS Act readiness assessments and SOCI uplift programs targeting Tier 2/3 Defence primes and their SME supply chains.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Defence and National Security partners this fortnight to mobilise a combined SOCI review-response and SAMS Act personnel security offering, and convene the AIDN-aligned SME bench to pitch compliance uplift packages before primes flow down new obligations.

Commonwealth

AI Brief22 articles

State-level enforcement remains active, with SA Health continuing its tobacco retailer crackdown in Adelaide and the SA government signalling firearm reform that is already creating commercial uncertainty for licensed retailers. Federal Budget 2026-27 measures (NDIS, migration, security funding, Bondi response) will cascade into state compliance and service-delivery obligations, while joint Commonwealth-ACT-NSW infrastructure investment in the Canberra-Sydney rail corridor signals cross-border planning and procurement workstreams. Commonwealth regulator focus (ASIC governance enforcement, ASIC/ACCC greenwashing, Federal Court Tickle v Giggle ruling on gender identity discrimination) will flow through to state-regulated entities, equal-opportunity frameworks and licensee governance. Advisory opportunities: SA retail licensing/AML-style compliance uplift, ACT/NSW infrastructure delivery governance, and reviewing state anti-discrimination policies post-Tickle.

New South Wales

AI Brief32 articles

NSW regulatory activity is dominated by enforcement and integrity actions: ICAC has opened 'Pink Ops' public hearings into Parramatta Council over workplace surveillance and cronyism, while SafeWork NSW has launched concurrent psychosocial investigations into Bluesfest and Married At First Sight, signalling expanding use of psychosocial WHS powers against employers and production companies. The NSW Auditor-General's excoriating report on $1.3bn in Visiting Medical Officer payments exposes Health Department control failures and creates immediate remediation, assurance and payment-controls advisory demand. Compliance pressure is also rising through gambling-harm venue inspections, a $40m firearms registry uplift ahead of the Bondi audit, ongoing 'good character' licensing reforms, and Transport NSW workforce mobility disputes — all pointing to advisory opportunities in WHS/psychosocial risk frameworks, public-sector financial controls, probity, and licensing compliance.

Victoria

AI Brief36 articles

Victoria is rolling out a dense slate of new regulatory regimes and enforcement activity: the Social Services Regulator and new Child Safety Regulator are bedding in (the latter already facing governance scrutiny), toughened building regulation reforms are advancing, and proposed OHS amendments will reset employer duties. The Auditor-General has opened a probe into the 40-year Lottery Corporation licence extension, the education watchdog has launched a fresh schools investigation, and a heavy-vehicle compliance blitz signals stepped-up operational enforcement. The state budget pairs debt pressure with funding for community safety and child outcomes, while private schools face a 2027 student-device restriction and Gellung Warl is established under Victoria's first Treaty. Advisory opportunities cluster around OHS readiness, building compliance, social services and child safety regulator engagement, and probity/value-for-money reviews of major state contracts.

Queensland

AI Brief31 articles

Queensland has launched a parliamentary inquiry into federal environmental laws while simultaneously fast-tracking major coal and gold project approvals, signalling a more interventionist state approvals posture that will reshape proponent compliance pathways and EPBC interface risk. The CFMEU inquiry is intensifying with evidence that union leaders had influence over the former state government and workplace safety appointments, creating significant exposure for construction sector clients and prompting likely procurement, probity and WHS regulator reforms. ECEC enforcement remains active with fresh penalties under the National Quality Framework (Kidi Kingdom $12,000 fine) alongside regulator data showing shifting service-mix obligations for approved providers. State fuel security policy is escalating via the Taroom Trough development standoff with the Commonwealth, opening advisory work around resources approvals, royalties and critical infrastructure resilience.

Western Australia

AI Brief33 articles

WA is executing a broad multi-sector regulatory reset: new illicit tobacco/vape laws have already triggered the first store closures, while major rental reforms (banning no-grounds evictions) and homebuilding consumer protections impose fresh compliance obligations on landlords, agents and builders. Significant regulatory restructuring is underway for renewable energy project approvals, government data governance (with a new expert oversight panel), and mining rules (temporarily eased during the fuel crisis), alongside enforcement activity including fuel price-setting fines against ~80 service stations and federal scrutiny of Alcoa's Willowdale clearing. Strategically, leaked documents suggest WA is preparing to abandon its emissions reduction targets, and the state remains under pressure to close a child safety loophole — both creating advisory exposure around ESG positioning, working-with-children compliance, and reputational risk. Priority advisory opportunities sit in retail/tobacco licensing readiness, residential tenancy and building compliance uplift, renewables approvals strategy, and data governance frameworks aligned to the new reform panel.

  • Big changes for regulation of WA renewable energy projects - Ashurst Ashurst · 14 May 2026, 07:31 PM

South Australia

AI Brief23 articles

South Australia is overhauling its fuel pricing and supply laws, introducing emergency powers and on-the-spot fines following a blitz that penalised nine petrol stations — creating immediate compliance obligations for fuel retailers and advisory demand on pricing disclosure regimes. SafeWork SA continues active WHS enforcement, including a $78,000 fine for a guardrail collapse, alongside a new $25,000 safety grants program signalling regulator priorities around proactive risk controls. The SA Government's renewal of Serco's Adelaide Remand Centre contract amid union opposition highlights ongoing scrutiny of outsourced custodial services and procurement governance. SA health leaders are also publicly opposing federal gambling reforms, indicating potential state-level policy divergence and stakeholder engagement opportunities in harm-minimisation regulation.

Tasmania

AI Brief10 articles

Tasmania is progressing a dense legislative agenda with direct compliance implications: the Mandatory Notifications Bill and Building Amendment Bill 2026 have passed the House of Assembly, government contracting rules are being reformed, and consultation has opened on amendments to the Aboriginal Heritage Act. Enforcement and accountability pressure is intensifying around child safety, with a damning report on government failures and revelations Tasmania Police disputed its mandatory reporting obligations — signalling heightened regulator scrutiny of public sector compliance frameworks. Commonwealth EPBC reform exposes the state to potential native logging compensation liabilities of up to $300m, while sector-specific measures (national park influencer permits, delayed greyhound racing ban, local government consolidation) reshape operating conditions. Advisory opportunities cluster around mandatory reporting uplift, procurement compliance redesign, Aboriginal heritage due diligence, and building regulation transition support.

Australian Capital Territory

AI Brief18 articles

The ACT Government is advancing a significant reform agenda, progressing protections-and-transparency legislation alongside flagged retirement living reforms positioning the sector for a larger role in housing supply. A parliamentary inquiry has recommended licensing of strata management workforces, signalling a new occupational licensing regime advisory firms should prepare clients for. Access Canberra is under scrutiny following findings it acted unlawfully in a Working With Vulnerable People (WWVP) refusal, while separately defending its Big Splash enforcement decision — indicating heightened administrative-law and procedural fairness risk for the regulator. The ACT also ranked top (with Queensland) for auditor-general independence, reinforcing a robust public-sector accountability environment relevant to government clients.

Northern Territory

AI Brief27 articles

The NT Government has introduced significant legislative reforms across multiple sectors, including a new bill to accelerate energy project investment, exploration reforms targeting the extractive/mining sector, and contentious amendments to child protection laws that weaken the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. Following a preventable childcare death, the government has accepted sweeping reforms and ordered an independent review of the child protection system, though First Nations and Children's Commissioners have criticised the lack of consultation. WorkSafe NT has launched a probe into PALM scheme worker housing conditions, signalling heightened workplace regulator scrutiny. Advisory opportunities exist around resources/energy approvals pathways, child safety compliance frameworks for care providers, and employer obligations for seasonal worker accommodation.

Clients investing in

Tech & AI

Regulators have shifted from AI principles to AI enforcement this fortnight: ASIC and APRA have written directly to boards on emerging AI risks, OAIC is consulting on Automated Decision Making transparency guidance, and WGEA has published its own AI transparency statement — setting the disclosure benchmark every regulated entity will be measured against. ASIC's 26-092MR call for urgent cyber uplift as AI accelerates threats, paired with Transgrid's Operational Technology security roadmap at the AER, confirms cyber-AI convergence is now the dominant board-level risk lens. On the spend side, the Commonwealth is sustaining major tech programs with fresh billions, NSW is tipping another $500m into its core policing platform after the Bezos-backed vendor was dumped, $40m into the firearms registry ahead of Bondi and COPS audit fallout, and WA has stood up an expert panel to oversee whole-of-government data reform. AUSTRAC's Financial Integrity Hub and ASIC's digital guidance overhaul both point to regulator-side platform builds where KPMG's Powered Enterprise and Trusted AI assets land directly. The NSW auditor-general's excoriation of VMO IT controls in Health is a live remediation lane. Closest KPMG capability: Trusted AI governance, AI assurance against the OAIC/APRA/ASIC stack, cyber-OT uplift, and large-scale core-system replacement advisory.

Australian Securities and Investments CommissionAustralian Energy RegulatorAustralian Prudential Regulation Authority

Implications for KPMG: Brief the NSW Government and Financial Services partners this fortnight on a joint Trusted AI + cyber-OT uplift pursuit targeting ASIC, APRA, AUSTRAC and NSW Police before the OAIC ADM guidance closes and the $500m policing core-system reset goes to market.

Show clients (3)
  • Australian Securities and Investments Commission commonwealth

    ASIC is simultaneously running a digital and guidance overhaul to cut regulatory clutter and issuing joint letters with APRA on emerging AI governance risks, capped by 26-092MR calling for urgent cyber uplift as AI accelerates threat velocity. This converges three high-margin advisory pulls — AI governance frameworks, cyber resilience uplift, and regtech-enabled guidance redesign — into a single regulator-mandated buying window across ASIC-regulated entities.

    Implications: Brief the Financial Services and Cyber partners this fortnight on a packaged ASIC-aligned AI governance, cyber uplift and regtech offer aimed at boards of listed entities, AFSL holders and APRA-regulated dual-regulated clients.

This opens pull-through for KPMG on OT/IT cyber assurance, CPA economic justification, and regulatory submission support as other TNSPs and DNSPs line up similar security roadmaps.

    Implications: Brief the Energy & Natural Resources partner this fortnight on Transgrid's OT Security Roadmap CPA to pre-position KPMG cyber and regulatory economics teams ahead of the next wave of AER OT security determinations.

  • Australian Prudential Regulation Authority commonwealth

    APRA has issued a joint letter with ASIC to regulated entities flagging emerging AI risks and setting governance expectations across model risk, data integrity, and third-party model oversight (per Corrs Chambers Westgarth coverage). This signals an imminent uplift cycle across ADIs, insurers and superannuation trustees, opening pull-through for AI governance frameworks, CPS 230/234 alignment, and model-risk assurance mandates.

    Implications: Brief the Banking and Insurance regulatory partners this fortnight on a co-branded APRA AI-governance readiness diagnostic targeting Tier-1 ADIs and major insurers ahead of supervisory engagement.

Capability reviews

Capability reviews are now the operating mode across Commonwealth and state public sectors, and the signals this fortnight are converging fast. ATEC has cleared the House and received its Letter of Expectations, instantly creating a stand-up agency with no operating model, target operating design, or assurance framework — a clean-sheet brief KPMG should be writing. The Federal Budget 2026 is simultaneously redrawing APS headcount envelopes across multiple portfolios, and a new federal counter-terror role signals Home Affairs and AGD capability re-baselining. State-side, NSW Transport's mobility blowout exposes a workforce transition failure that PEPI's people-and-change bench can remediate, while WA's expert panel on government data reforms and the NT child protection inquiry led by Karen Webb open assurance and capability-uplift seats. AHPRA's direction to 'immediately improve' investigation timeliness and quality is a regulator-on-regulator capability review — the exact territory where our Regulatory Transformation playbook lands. ASPI's critique of NDS 2026 force-design balance also flags a Defence capability mix review that the Canberra partners should be positioning against now.

Australian Health Practitioner Regulation AgencyDepartment of DefenceDepartment of Finance

Implications for KPMG: Brief the Education and Federal Government partners this fortnight on a joint ATEC stand-up pursuit — target operating model, assurance framework and workforce design — and lock in meetings with the ATEC Commissioner and DEWR Deputy Secretary before the December operating-model decision point.

Show clients (3)
  • Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency commonwealth

    AHPRA has been formally directed to immediately improve the timeliness and quality of its practitioner investigations, signalling an externally-mandated capability uplift across its notifications and complaints-handling function. This creates a defined window for KPMG to pull through case-management process redesign, investigator workforce capability, and quality-assurance frameworks under a regulator-imposed deadline.

    Implications: Brief the Commonwealth health regulator partner this fortnight on a targeted AHPRA play covering investigations operating-model redesign, investigator capability uplift, and QA/assurance framework build to evidence the ordered improvements.

    • AHPRA ordered to ‘immediately improve’ investigation timeliness, quality - Medical Republic 2026-05-06T15:36:05+10:00
  • Department of Defence commonwealth

    Department of Defence is being pressed via ASPI's NDS 2026 critique to broaden its capability review lens beyond high-intensity conventional conflict to re-weight peacekeeping, HADR and lower-tier contingencies, signalling a reopening of force-structure and capability-mix assumptions. This matters because any reframing of NDS priorities cascades into program reviews, workforce planning and industry policy work where KPMG can pull through strategy, operating-model and assurance advisory.

    Implications: Brief the Defence partner this fortnight on positioning KPMG for capability-mix and force-design review support as NDS 2026 assumptions come under public challenge.

    • NDS 2026 – Focus on high-intensity conflict neglects peacekeeping and HADR 2026-05-18T06:00:42+10:00
  • Department of Finance commonwealth

    The Department of Finance is driving the resourcing and ASL settings for the APS through Budget 2026, with The Mandarin reporting material ups and downs across federal agencies that will recalibrate capability footprints sector by sector. This puts Finance at the centre of capability uplift decisions across the Commonwealth, creating immediate pull-through for KPMG workforce design, target operating model and capability review offerings as agencies absorb the staffing verdicts.

    Implications: Brief the Commonwealth Government partner this fortnight on Pursuing Department of Finance with a Budget 2026 capability-uplift play, packaging KPMG's TOM, workforce planning and APS capability review credentials for the agencies most exposed to ASL shifts.

    • Budget 2026: Federal agency ups and downs 2026-05-14T07:24:10+10:00

Learning & development

NSW is running an active interstate poaching campaign to fix a structural police workforce shortfall, with Premier Minns conceding the numbers problem and signalling sustained recruitment investment. The buyer here is NSW Police and the Department of Premier and Cabinet, with Treasury underwriting the package — meaning attraction, relocation, accelerated lateral-entry training and retention economics are all in play simultaneously. The signal for L&D is that recruitment alone will not close the gap; NSW will need a re-engineered lateral-transfer academy pathway, recognition-of-prior-service frameworks, and leadership pipeline rebuild to absorb out-of-state intake. KPMG's People & Change and Workforce Strategy benches are closest, with direct precedent in Defence lateral-entry design and ADF retention modelling that ports cleanly to a uniformed-service context. Expect Victoria and Queensland to retaliate with their own retention plays once attrition data lands, opening a multi-jurisdictional workforce-economics conversation. The window is now — before the FY26-27 NSW budget locks the program shape.

Implications for KPMG: Brief the NSW Government partner this fortnight on a lateral-entry academy and retention-economics pursuit into NSW Police and DPC, leveraging our Defence workforce credentials, before the FY26-27 budget bilaterals close out the program design.

Risk & assurance

Regulators are moving from policy settings to active enforcement across Risk & assurance, and the volume of activity in this fortnight is unusually concentrated. The ACNC has suspended the RACP president-elect over governance and safety concerns, signalling that charity and not-for-profit boards now face live regulator-led intervention — every health college, peak body and large NFP client should be reassessing conflicts, overseas-operations controls and director fitness against the ACNC's published decision logic. In parallel, the NDIS Commission and DSS are pushing providers out of Territory markets while Government readies legislation tightening access tests, putting fraud controls, eligibility assurance and provider-integrity uplift squarely on the NDIA, DSS and state disability agency agendas. AUSTRAC's 2026 money-laundering update and VASP registration regime, TGA's listed-medicines compliance sweep, Ahpra's anti-racism framework and ATO/Border Force illicit-tobacco prosecutions together mark a step-change in regulator appetite for evidence-based assurance over attestation. Active clients this fortnight are AUSTRAC, NDIS Commission, NDIA, DSS, ACNC, TGA, Ahpra and the NT Government, each procuring or signalling demand for forensic, controls-testing and regulatory-operating-model work. KPMG's Forensic, Risk Consulting, NFP governance and Health & Human Services teams are the closest fit, with the AUSTRAC VASP rollout and the NDIS provider clean-up the two most immediate, scoped opportunities.

Australian Securities and Investments CommissionAustralian Transaction Reports and Analysis CentreAustralian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission

Implications for KPMG: Brief the Forensic and NFP Governance partners this fortnight to mobilise a joint pursuit on AUSTRAC's VASP registration uplift and an ACNC-aligned board-assurance offer to RACP-tier colleges and large NFP clients before AUSTRAC's 2026 guidance and the NDIS access-test legislation land.

Show clients (3)
  • Australian Securities and Investments Commission commonwealth

    ASIC is widening its assurance perimeter simultaneously across finfluencer enforcement, joint AI-governance expectations with APRA, greenwashing surveillance with the ACCC, auditor and MIS audit-quality reviews, and early observations on mandatory sustainability reporting ahead of 30 June 2026. This compresses multiple second-line uplift programs into a single 12-month window, creating concurrent demand for controls design, AI risk frameworks, sustainability assurance readiness and audit-quality remediation across regulated populations.

    Implications: Brief the ASIC and Financial Services partners this fortnight on a bundled 'ASIC 2026 readiness' play linking AFSL conduct controls, AI governance attestation, AASB S2 assurance dry-runs and MIS/auditor remediation into one sequenced pursuit.

    AUSTRAC has published its 2026 money laundering and proliferation financing updates alongside a refreshed national financial-crime risk snapshot, and has opened a formal enforcement investigation into Tabcorp's AML/CTF compliance.     Implications: Brief the Financial Services and Gaming sector partners this fortnight on AUSTRAC's enforcement posture and pitch independent AML/CTF program reviews, VASP registration readiness and Part 8 remediation support to exposed reporting entities.

    • Registration questions for virtual asset service provider - AUSTRAC 2026-05-13T08:36:13+10:00
    • AUSTRAC releases updated risk snapshot of Australia’s financial crime landscape - AUSTRAC 2026-05-12T22:00:00+10:00
    • Proliferation financing update 2026 - AUSTRAC 2026-05-12T11:57:07+10:00
    • Austrac to investigate Tabcorp’s AML/CTF compliance - Club Management 2026-05-12T08:23:29+10:00
  • Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission commonwealth

    The ACNC has exercised its enforcement powers to suspend the RACP president-elect Dr Sharmila Chandran over governance and safety concerns, signalling a sharper appetite for board-level intervention alongside fresh guidance on managing conflicts of interest in overseas operations. This escalation lifts the bar for charity board assurance, governance frameworks and conflicts management — opening pull-through for KPMG governance reviews, responsible-person assurance and remediation support across the NFP and health-college sector.

    Implications: Brief the NFP and Health sector partners this fortnight on positioning KPMG for board governance health-checks and conflicts-of-interest framework uplifts across ACNC-registered medical colleges and large charities.

    • Leadership Crisis at RACP: President-Elect Suspended by ACNC - Azat TV 2026-05-20T04:00:00+10:00
    • Dr Sharmila Chandran RACP Suspension May 20: Regulator Halts Presidency - Meyka 2026-05-20T03:00:54+10:00
    • Charities Regulator Suspends President-Elect of Medical College Amid Safety Concerns - SSBCrack 2026-05-19T18:32:02+10:00
    • Charities commission suspends RACP president-elect - Medical Republic 2026-05-19T15:05:05+10:00
    • De-identified registration decision summary – addressing conflicts of interest when working overseas - ACNC 2026-05-13T04:13:14+10:00
No investment signal this fortnight. The next refresh will rebuild theme cards from new article classifications.

Implications for KPMG

Brief the NSW Government and Financial Services partners this fortnight on a joint Trusted AI + cyber-OT uplift pursuit targeting ASIC, APRA, AUSTRAC and NSW Police before the OAIC ADM guidance closes and the $500m policing core-system reset goes to market. Brief the Education and Federal Government partners this fortnight on a joint ATEC stand-up pursuit — target operating model, assurance framework and workforce design — and lock in meetings with the ATEC Commissioner and DEWR Deputy Secretary before the December operating-model decision point. Brief the NSW Government partner this fortnight on a lateral-entry academy and retention-economics pursuit into NSW Police and DPC, leveraging our Defence workforce credentials, before the FY26-27 budget bilaterals close out the program design. Brief the Forensic and NFP Governance partners this fortnight to mobilise a joint pursuit on AUSTRAC's VASP registration uplift and an ACNC-aligned board-assurance offer to RACP-tier colleges and large NFP clients before AUSTRAC's 2026 guidance and the NDIS access-test legislation land.

  • Tech & AI: Brief the NSW Government and Financial Services partners this fortnight on a joint Trusted AI + cyber-OT uplift pursuit targeting ASIC, APRA, AUSTRAC and NSW Police before the OAIC ADM guidance closes and the $500m policing core-system reset goes to market.
  • Capability reviews: Brief the Education and Federal Government partners this fortnight on a joint ATEC stand-up pursuit — target operating model, assurance framework and workforce design — and lock in meetings with the ATEC Commissioner and DEWR Deputy Secretary before the December operating-model decision point.
  • Learning & development: Brief the NSW Government partner this fortnight on a lateral-entry academy and retention-economics pursuit into NSW Police and DPC, leveraging our Defence workforce credentials, before the FY26-27 budget bilaterals close out the program design.
  • Risk & assurance: Brief the Forensic and NFP Governance partners this fortnight to mobilise a joint pursuit on AUSTRAC's VASP registration uplift and an ACNC-aligned board-assurance offer to RACP-tier colleges and large NFP clients before AUSTRAC's 2026 guidance and the NDIS access-test legislation land.
Implications will populate once theme synthesis runs in the next pipeline.

Regulation, regulator-hosted & L&D events

Upcoming events relevant to PSLG Leaders — regulator briefings, AICD/IPAA capability sessions, and L&D opportunities. Auto-refreshed each pipeline run; manual entries in context/events.json are the source of truth.

Event type
Regulation event
Regulator-hosted
L&D

Week of 2026-05-22

  • 2026-05-22
    AICD Boards & Regulators Lunch — Sydney
    AICDL&Dpublic_sectorSydney

    Partner-grade conversation between board chairs and regulator leadership on regulator expectations of director duty-holders. Useful BD touchpoint for FS and infrastructure pipeline.

Week of 2026-05-27

  • 2026-05-27
    All Actuaries Summit 2026
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    All Actuaries Summit 2026

Week of 2026-06-04

  • 2026-06-04
    VIC Cyber Regulator Industry Briefing
    Office of the Victorian Information CommissionerRegulator-hostedpublic_sectorMelbourne

    Victorian information-protection regulator briefing on cyber-incident notification expectations and supervisory direction for state-funded entities. Brief VIC public-sector partner.

Week of 2026-06-12

  • 2026-06-12
    APRA Financial Risk & Resilience Practitioners Roundtable — Q3
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    APRA-led roundtable with FAR-impacted entities; signal on operational resilience supervisory priorities for FY27. Brief FS partners ahead of bank/insurer mid-year reviews.

Week of 2026-06-16

  • 2026-06-16
    2026 AFR Insurance Summit
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

  • 2026-06-26
    ACCC Compliance & Enforcement Forum
    ACCCRegulator-hostedpublic_sectorMelbourne

    ACCC-hosted compliance forum covering FY27 enforcement priorities (digital platforms, unfair trading, supermarkets). Pursue brief for retail, telco, and digital partners.

Week of 2026-07-09

  • 2026-07-09
    ASIC Enforcement Update Webinar — Q3 2026
    ASICRegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    Quarterly enforcement priorities readout from ASIC Commissioners. Direct read into FS and listed-company supervisory tone for the next 6 months.

Week of 2026-07-23

  • 2026-07-23
    KPMG Regulatory Practice Masterclass — RT CoP
    KPMGL&Dpublic_sectorOnline

    Internal CoP masterclass on duty-holder experience and risk-based regulation, drawing on current PSLG engagements. Partner/Director attendance encouraged.

Week of 2026-08-13

  • 2026-08-13 – 2026-08-14
    IPAA National Regulatory Governance Forum 2026
    IPAARegulation eventpublic_sectorCanberra

    Two-day national forum on regulatory stewardship, RPPPF practice, and cross-jurisdictional regulator capability. Strong PSLG attendance expected; CoP-relevant.

Week of 2026-08-20

  • 2026-08-20
    AHRI Public Sector L&D Symposium
    AHRIL&Dpublic_sectorSydney

    AHRI symposium on public-sector workforce capability and regulator L&D investment. Useful read on capability-reviews market for NSW/Cwlth pipeline.

No upcoming events match these filters. Check back after the next refresh.

Showing 0 regulators

State-gov priorities

Cited strategic priorities across all 9 jurisdictions, refreshed bi-annually. Every source URL is .gov.au.

Housing supply dominates as a near-universal commitment, with VIC (800,000 homes by 2034), QLD (1 million by 2046), NSW (170,000+ via TOD), ACT (30,000 by 2030), TAS (10,000 affordable by 2032) and SA ($843.6M in Budget 25-26) all setting hard targets, creating consistent demand for delivery assurance, developer-government partnering models, and social/affordable housing program design that KPMG can package as a repeatable PEPI offering. A second clear pattern is industrial sovereignty and clean-energy transition — Commonwealth's Future Made in Australia aligns tightly with SA's State Prosperity Project and Hydrogen Action Plan, TAS's 200% renewable target, WA's Made in WA / Strategic Industries Fund, NSW's manufacturing reshoring, and VIC's Breakthrough Victoria — opening bids around supply-chain analysis, critical minerals strategy, and grant-program assurance. Health and workforce reform is the third axis, with CTH Strengthening Medicare and Aged Care transition, NSW safe staffing rollout, VIC Royal Commission mental-health implementation, TAS Healthcare 2040, and SA's $2.5B health package all requiring workforce planning, change management and outcomes-measurement capability. Divergence is sharpest between resource-led jurisdictions (NT's energy/defence/mining/agriculture pivot and QLD's 30-year QRIDP) and the demand-side, services-heavy agendas of VIC and ACT, where wellbeing budgeting, Treaty, and social-licence work dominate — a useful segmentation for tailoring KPMG go-to-market messages. Public-safety and integrity reform also splits the field, with QLD's "adult crime, adult time" and NT's Crime Reduction Strategy taking a hard enforcement posture while NSW Safer Pathway and VIC Treaty emphasise community-led and rights-based responses, both creating stakeholder-consultation and evaluation opportunities. Finally, most jurisdictions are operating under refreshed 20-year infrastructure strategies (Infrastructure SA, Infrastructure WA, Sydney Metro, ACT $8.1B IIP, Bruce Highway) alongside APS-style capability reform at the Commonwealth — a strong lane for KPMG program assurance, gateway reviews, and public-sector capability uplift. The combined pattern points to three priority BD plays: housing-delivery PMO and assurance; energy-transition and sovereign-industry advisory; and integrated health/workforce transformation, each anchored by stakeholder consultation services that translate across at least five jurisdictions.

Last refreshed 2026-04-22. Bi-annual schedule; source URLs verified to .gov.au.

Commonwealth

  • Future Made in Australia
    The Treasury / Department of Industry, Science and Resources 2024-2034 (10-year, $22.7B package)
    Program design, sector assessment frameworks, investment mandate design, industry consultation, economic modelling, community benefit evaluation
    Source (2024-05-14)
  • Strengthening Medicare
    Department of Health, Disability and Ageing 2023-2027 (reform rolling); Budget 2025-26 expansion from Nov 2025
    Primary care reform evaluation, Urgent Care Clinic rollout support, workforce modelling, digital health implementation, Medicare integrity assurance
    Source (2024-05-14)
  • 2024 National Defence Strategy and Integrated Investment Program
    Department of Defence 2024-2034 ($330B Integrated Investment Program to 2033-34)
    Capability program assurance, workforce strategy, sovereign industrial capability priorities, AUKUS implementation support, capability reviews
    Source (2024-04-17)
  • APS Reform Agenda
    Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet / Australian Public Service Commission 2022-ongoing (4 pillars, 59 initiatives; Public Service Amendment Act 2024 enacted)
    Integrity and capability reviews, workforce strategy, stewardship framework design, data & digital workforce planning, contractor-to-APS conversion support
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • Working for Women: A Strategy for Gender Equality
    Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet (Office for Women) 2024-2034 (10-year strategy)
    Gender impact analysis, workplace gender equality evaluation, Government Board appointment strategy support, pay-gap economic modelling, consultation design
    Source (2024-03-07)
  • Aged Care reform – transition to Support at Home
    Department of Health, Disability and Ageing Aged Care Act 2024 commenced 1 Nov 2025; CHSP transition to Support at Home by 1 Jul 2027
    Provider transition support, data and digital strategy implementation, workforce modelling, single assessment system evaluation, consumer journey design
    Source (2024-07-01)

New South Wales

  • Safe Staffing Levels in NSW public hospitals
    NSW Ministry of Health Phase one early 2024; full rollout 2,480 FTE nurses/midwives by June 2027
    Workforce modelling, implementation assurance, change management support, clinical governance advisory, rostering system review
    Source (2024-03-11)
  • Transport Oriented Development Program (TOD)
    Department of Planning, Housing and Infrastructure TOD planning controls commenced 13 May 2024; 15-year horizon; 170,000+ homes
    Precinct planning, inclusionary zoning advisory, infrastructure funding strategy, development feasibility, stakeholder consultation
    Source (2024-05-13)
  • Sydney Metro – Western Sydney Airport and Sydney Metro West
    Transport for NSW / Sydney Metro Western Sydney Airport line opens 2026; Metro West completion by 2032; network to 46 stations by 2030
    Program assurance, capability reviews, cost estimation reviews, land use integration, precinct economic analysis
    Source (2024-06-01)
  • Bringing back manufacturing to NSW (state-owned energy and rollingstock)
    Cabinet Office / Treasury NSW / Transport for NSW 2023-2027+ (current Parliament)
    Industrial strategy advisory, local content analysis, state-owned corporation establishment, procurement strategy, workforce development
    Source (2024-09-01)
  • NSW Budget 2025-26 priorities (essential services + diversified economy)
    NSW Treasury 2025-26 forward estimates to 2028-29
    Fiscal strategy advisory, expenditure review, program evaluation, revenue analysis, investment prioritisation
    Source (2025-06-24)
  • Safer Pathway – domestic and family violence response
    Department of Communities and Justice Statewide rollout ongoing; DFV reforms 2024-ongoing
    Service design, outcomes evaluation, risk assessment framework review, cross-agency data sharing advisory, program logic review
    Source (2024-01-01)

Victoria

  • Victoria's Housing Statement – The Decade Ahead 2024-2034
    Department of Transport and Planning 2024-2034 (800,000 homes / 80,000 per year target)
    Planning reform advisory, activity centre masterplans, affordable housing economics, surplus land unlocking, stakeholder consultation, LGA target negotiations
    Source (2023-09-20)
  • Plan for Victoria (long-term plan to 2051)
    Department of Transport and Planning Released Feb 2025; horizon to 2051 (2.24 million homes target)
    LGA housing target analysis, settlement pattern modelling, sustainability and climate policy integration, First Peoples self-determination in planning, infrastructure sequencing
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Mental Health and Wellbeing reform (Royal Commission implementation)
    Department of Health 2021-2031 (decade of reform); Statewide Service and Capital Plan 2024-2037
    Service design, capital planning, workforce strategy, outcomes evaluation, lived experience engagement design, Mental Health Levy economic analysis
    Source (2024-07-01)
  • Treaty for Victoria / First Peoples' self-determination
    First Peoples – State Relations Statewide Treaty Bill 2025 introduced; negotiations ongoing
    Closing the Gap accountability framework design, service commissioning transition, policy impact analysis, governance advisory for new representative body
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Breakthrough Victoria Fund – research and innovation commercialisation
    Department of Jobs, Skills, Industry and Regions 2020-2030 ($2B 10-year fund; $1.3B co-investment leveraged by 2024)
    Commercialisation strategy, innovation ecosystem review, investment thesis advisory, co-investment deal structuring, industry sector analysis
    Source (2024-06-30)

Queensland

  • Making Queensland Safer – adult crime, adult time youth justice reform
    Department of Youth Justice and Victim Support (renamed 1 Nov 2024) Making Queensland Safer Act 2024 assented 13 Dec 2024; Queensland Youth Justice Strategy 2024-2028
    Program logic review, early intervention evaluation, victim advocacy service design, circuit breaker sentencing assurance, regional reset program evaluation
    Source (2024-08-01)
  • Homes for Queenslanders housing plan
    Department of Housing and Public Works 2024-2046 (1 million new homes by 2046; 53,500 social homes)
    Housing target modelling, modular housing economics, social housing procurement, church/charity land strategy, planning reform advisory
    Source (2024-02-14)
  • A Fresh Start – cost-of-living and tax relief
    Queensland Treasury 100 Day Plan completed early 2025; ongoing measures 2024-2028
    Revenue impact modelling, cost-of-living measure evaluation, payroll tax analysis, first home buyer stamp duty modelling
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Queensland Resources Industry Development Plan (QRIDP)
    Department of Natural Resources and Mines, Manufacturing and Regional and Rural Development 2022-2052 (30-year vision; living document)
    Critical minerals strategy, decarbonisation roadmaps, regional community transition planning, ESG performance frameworks, royalty policy analysis
    Source (2024-10-01)
  • Bruce Highway Advisory Council and regional infrastructure
    Department of Transport and Main Roads Re-established 100 Day Plan period (late 2024); ongoing through current term
    Regional corridor prioritisation, cost-benefit analysis, safety-focused project business cases, stakeholder engagement with regional councils
    Source (2024-11-30)
  • Department of the Premier and Cabinet Strategic Plan 2024-2028
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (Queensland) 2024-2028 (current through 30 April 2025 review; superseded by 2025-2029 plan)
    Central agency capability review, Cabinet support modernisation, policy quality uplift, intergovernmental strategy, corporate excellence frameworks
    Source (2024-06-28)

Western Australia

  • WA Government Priorities 2025-2029 (Jobs, Health, Housing, Community, Environment, Infrastructure and Services)
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (WA) 2025-2029 (current parliamentary term)
    Cross-agency delivery coordination, outcomes-based budgeting advisory, capability reviews for new departments, program assurance
    Source (2025-03-01)
  • Made in WA Plan – $1B Strategic Industries Fund
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation 2025-2035 ($500M additional investment; SIF total $1B over 10 years)
    Industrial policy advisory, biomedical precinct economics, battery manufacturing supply chain, EV bus and ferry procurement, screen industry strategy
    Source (2025-05-08)
  • Defence West: WA Defence and Defence Industry Strategy 2025
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation / Defence West 2025-2040 (15-year strategic vision)
    AUKUS readiness, continuous naval shipbuilding industrial strategy, defence workforce planning, supplier diversification, sovereign capability analysis
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Diversify WA: 2024 Update (economic development framework)
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation Launched 2019; updated 2024; nine external-facing sectors
    Sector prioritisation, investment attraction strategy, comparative advantage analysis, export market development, regulatory environment review
    Source (2024-09-01)
  • State Infrastructure Strategy (Infrastructure WA)
    Infrastructure WA 20-year strategy; IWA Annual Report and Government Response
    Infrastructure business case review, prioritisation methodology, capital program assurance, decarbonisation of infrastructure, asset recycling analysis
    Source (2024-01-01)

South Australia

  • State Prosperity Project
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (SA) / Department for Energy and Mining Announced 2023; long-term (renewables, critical minerals, green manufacturing)
    Critical minerals strategy, green steel economics, Northern Water business case, green industrial precinct design, trade and investment attraction
    Source (2023-09-01)
  • South Australia's Net Zero Strategy 2024-2030
    Department for Environment and Water / Department for Energy and Mining 2024-2030 (100% net renewable electricity by 2027; net zero by 2050)
    Decarbonisation pathways, hydrogen industry business case, circular economy policy, climate risk disclosure, renewable energy grid modelling
    Source (2024-01-01)
  • Hydrogen Jobs Plan and South Australia's Hydrogen Action Plan
    Office of Hydrogen Power South Australia (OHPSA) $593M green hydrogen power facility; world-leading hydrogen facilities 2026; 20 actions across 5 themes
    Hydrogen offtake structuring, green hydrogen cost modelling, project finance advisory, regulatory pathway analysis under HRE Act
    Source (2024-01-01)
  • SA State Budget 2025-26: housing, health, cost of living, skills
    South Australian Department of Treasury and Finance 2025-26 forward estimates; $2.5B health over 5 years; $843.6M housing
    Fiscal strategy advisory, housing project economics (Playford Alive East, Seaton), mental health co-responder evaluation, skills investment evaluation
    Source (2025-06-05)
  • 20-Year State Infrastructure Strategy
    Infrastructure SA 20-year horizon; update cycles every 5 years
    Business case assurance, infrastructure prioritisation methodology, asset management strategy, capital program reviews
    Source (2024-01-01)

Tasmania

  • 2030 Strong Plan for Tasmania's Future
    Department of Premier and Cabinet (Tasmania) 2024-2030 (delivered via 2024-25 Budget and onwards)
    Whole-of-government program delivery assurance, cross-cutting priorities alignment, strategic investment review, outcome reporting frameworks
    Source (2024-09-12)
  • Long-Term Plan for Healthcare in Tasmania 2040 / Our Healthcare Future
    Department of Health (Tasmania) 2040 horizon; Our Healthcare Future released December 2022 with 6 action areas
    Primary/acute balance modelling, workforce strategy (especially rural/regional), integrated care pathways, digital health roadmap, demand modelling
    Source (2023-06-01)
  • Tasmanian Renewable Energy Target – 200% by 2040
    Department of State Growth / ReCFIT (Renewables, Climate and Future Industries Tasmania) 2020-2040 (200% renewable generation; Bell Bay green hydrogen hub by 2030)
    REZ business case support, Renewable Energy Approvals Pathway (REAP) process design, green hydrogen offtake analysis, transmission strategy, workforce planning
    Source (2024-11-01)
  • Tasmanian Housing Strategy – 10,000 affordable homes by 2032
    Department of State Growth / Homes Tasmania 2022-2032 ($550M over 4 years)
    Social housing project feasibility, community housing sector capacity, modular construction economics, land release strategy
    Source (2024-09-12)
  • Tasmanian Tourism Strategy – 1.6M interstate + 400K international visitors by 2030
    Department of State Growth / Tourism Tasmania 2024-2030 (target: $5.6B visitor spend)
    Visitor economy modelling, regional dispersal strategy, aviation access, destination marketing ROI, nature-based tourism economics
    Source (2024-12-01)

Australian Capital Territory

  • Parliamentary and Governing Agreement for the 10th ACT Legislative Assembly
    Chief Minister, Treasury and Economic Development Directorate (CMTEDD) 2024-2028 (10th Legislative Assembly term)
    Whole-of-government priority alignment, directorate capability reviews, outcome-based reporting framework design, reform program assurance
    Source (2024-10-20)
  • Housing for All – 30,000 new homes by 2030 and 5,000 new social/affordable/public homes
    Housing ACT / Chief Minister, Treasury and Economic Development Directorate 2024-2030 (30,000 homes; 5,000 social/affordable)
    Land release strategy, social housing procurement, Territory Plan implementation, affordable housing economics, construction pipeline modelling
    Source (2024-06-25)
  • 2024-25 Infrastructure Investment Program ($8B over 5 years)
    ACT Treasury / Infrastructure Canberra / Major Projects Canberra 2024-2029 ($1.4B in 2024-25; $8.1B over 5 years to 2028-29)
    Capital program assurance, business case reviews, PPP structuring advisory, precinct development, infrastructure decarbonisation
    Source (2024-06-25)
  • Light Rail Stage 2B (Commonwealth Park to Woden)
    Transport Canberra and City Services Directorate Draft EIS public comment July-Sept 2025; delivery subject to approvals
    Project assurance, heritage impact assessment, urban design advisory, National Triangle stakeholder engagement, wire-free technology economics
    Source (2024-07-07)
  • ACT Wellbeing Framework and 2024-25 Wellbeing Budget Statement
    Chief Minister, Treasury and Economic Development Directorate (CMTEDD) Framework operational since 2020; Budget-integrated from 2020-21 onwards
    Wellbeing-informed policy evaluation, outcome measurement, cost-benefit analysis incorporating wellbeing, priority population analysis, co-benefits assessment
    Source (2024-06-25)
  • ACT Climate Change Strategy 2026-2035 (under development) / Active Travel Plan 2024-30
    Environment, Planning and Sustainable Development Directorate New Climate Strategy 2026-2035 under development; Active Travel Plan 2024-2030; net zero emissions by 2045
    Emissions reduction pathways, transport decarbonisation, gas phase-out strategy, just transition planning, EV fleet procurement
    Source (2024-11-01)

Northern Territory

  • Rebuilding the Economy: Northern Territory Economic Strategy 2025
    Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet (NT) / Office of the Territory Coordinator 2025-2028 (5 strategic priorities; 20 strategic initiatives for immediate implementation)
    Major project approvals process design, investment attraction, regional growth prioritisation, Territory workforce strategy, industry sector strategies
    Source (2025-03-01)
  • Reducing Crime: Northern Territory Crime Reduction Strategy 2025
    Department of the Attorney-General and Justice / NT Police 2024-2027 (3-year strategy; review after 3 years)
    Criminal justice system reform evaluation, early intervention program design, youth diversion evaluation, data-driven policing analysis, bail reform impact assessment
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • Restoring the Territory Lifestyle Strategy
    Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet (NT) 2024-2028 (current parliamentary term)
    Parks/recreation asset management, community infrastructure investment, sporting facility modernisation, tariff subsidy economic analysis, cost of living measures
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • HomeGrown Territory and housing incentives package
    Department of Lands, Planning and the Environment / Treasury NT 2024-ongoing (current term)
    First home buyer grant economic analysis, construction capacity review, payroll tax relief modelling, land supply strategy
    Source (2025-01-01)
  • NT 2025-26 Budget priority sectors: energy, defence, agriculture, mining, tourism
    Northern Territory Treasury / Department of Trade, Business and Asian Relations 2025-26 forward estimates (4 years)
    Barossa gas project economics, Beetaloo Basin onshore gas analysis, cattle industry export modelling, defence precinct capability advisory, tourism recovery strategy
    Source (2025-05-13)
